title: Error while applying layer: ApplyLayer exit status 1 stdout:  stderr: lchown /opt/gitlab/sv/sshd/log/supervise/ok: no such file or directory
body:
Trying to build the following container: https://code.immerda.ch/immerda/container-images/gitlab/-/blob/2ffe4566ce1e3c9b3dd2bf02c766ddd699b6b1f4/Dockerfile.el7

This is failing with message in subject.

The gitlab runner runs on a CentOS 7.latest using docker (from CentOS). Runner is a privileged container and buildah containers use VFS, buildah runs as non-root.

When using buildah 1.16.5 in a CentOS 8 Stream container the build fails at the last step, when commiting the layer. I snapped the following messages:

```
level=debug msg="Start untar layer"
level=error msg="Error while applying layer: ApplyLayer exit status 1 stdout:  stderr: lchown /opt/gitlab/sv/sshd/log/supervise/ok: no such file or directory"
error committing container for step {Env:[PATH=/opt/gitlab/embedded/bin:/opt/gitlab/bin:/assets:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin TERM=xterm] Command:healthcheck Args:[CMD /opt/gitlab/bin/gitlab-healthcheck --fail --max-time 10] Flags:[--interval=60s --timeout=30s --retries=5] Attrs:map[] Message:HEALTHCHECK --interval=60s --timeout=30s --retries=5 CMD /opt/gitlab/bin/gitlab-healthcheck --fail --max-time 10 Original:HEALTHCHECK --interval=60s --timeout=30s --retries=5 CMD /opt/gitlab/bin/gitlab-healthcheck --fail --max-time 10}: error copying layers and metadata for container "e8c0ab28a6d779c0fe559685b71413eb2248648f4e506dc09a9c6c3ce9105f8e": Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:684666f89eeee5671330276541b2e08f96f7b28e7f675eee143e33e74af434b4": ApplyLayer exit status 1 stdout:  stderr: lchown /opt/gitlab/sv/sshd/log/supervise/ok: no such file or directory
level=debug msg="shutting down the store"
level=error msg="exit status 125"
```

See full output here: https://code.immerda.ch/immerda/container-images/gitlab/-/jobs/9809

When using buildah in a CentOS 7 image it works: https://code.immerda.ch/immerda/container-images/gitlab/-/jobs/9813

Buildah images are built here: https://code.immerda.ch/immerda/container-images/buildah

It used to work on CentOS (non-stream) 8, but fails since we moved our base images to CentOS Stream 8. I guess this is when buildah/podman/et.al. got updated.

```
bash-4.4$ buildah version
Version:         1.16.5
Go Version:      go1.15.2
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.5.2
Git Commit:      
Built:           Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
bash-4.4$ cat /etc/os-release 
NAME="CentOS Stream"
VERSION="8"
ID="centos"
ID_LIKE="rhel fedora"
VERSION_ID="8"
PLATFORM_ID="platform:el8"
PRETTY_NAME="CentOS Stream 8"
ANSI_COLOR="0;31"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:centos:centos:8"
HOME_URL="https://centos.org/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION="CentOS Stream"
bash-4.4$ rpm -qi buildah
Name        : buildah
Version     : 1.16.5
Release     : 4.module_el8.4.0+575+63b40ad7
Architecture: x86_64
Install Date: Thu Dec 10 10:04:52 2020
Group       : Unspecified
Size        : 27246512
License     : ASL 2.0
Signature   : RSA/SHA256, Tue Nov 10 23:12:31 2020, Key ID 05b555b38483c65d
Source RPM  : buildah-1.16.5-4.module_el8.4.0+575+63b40ad7.src.rpm
Build Date  : Tue Nov 10 22:15:36 2020
Build Host  : x86-01.mbox.centos.org
Relocations : (not relocatable)
Packager    : CentOS Buildsys <bugs@centos.org>
Vendor      : CentOS
URL         : https://buildah.io
Summary     : A command line tool used for creating OCI Images
Description :
The buildah package provides a command line tool which can be used to
* create a working container from scratch
or
* create a working container from an image as a starting point
* mount/umount a working container's root file system for manipulation
* save container's root file system layer to create a new image
* delete a working container or an image
```

comments.body: I have this same issue using `podman v2.2.0` and `buildah 1.18.0 (image-spec 1.0.1-dev, runtime-spec 1.0.2-dev)` on Debian, preventing me from deploying gitlab.

Could you make sure you have the latest fuse-overlayfs installed?

Here is what I use

$ rpm -q buildah fuse-overlayfs
buildah-1.18.0-1.fc33.x86_64
fuse-overlayfs-1.3.0-1.fc33.x86_64
I am building this within a container using VFS as storage backend, not sure how fuse-overlayfs is related to it?
Sorry I missed that.  I was thinking it could have been a fuse-overlayfs error, but if you are not using it...
@nalind Any ideas?  Looks like a file is disappearing?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@duritong Are you still seeing this issue?
I am not seeing it again with  `buildah-1.19.3-1.module_el8.4.0+673+eabfc99d.x86_64`
